title: Pain clinic referral documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.
assessment.rationale: Supports neuropathic-pain review, treatment response and specialist referral context for pain clinic referral documentation; no decision is generated.

source.title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings
source.population: Adults with neuropathic pain managed in non-specialist settings.
source.setting: Non-specialist assessment, treatment review, and specialist referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult neuropathic-pain review and treatment monitoring; partial for a generic neuropathy workflow and not directly validated for UAE prescribing or referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — assessment, treatment planning, referral, and review

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.

assessment.text: Supports functional pain assessment, complex pain or pain-service review, discharge communication, and clinician-arranged follow-up without autonomous treatment.
assessment.rationale: Supports complex-pain and pain-service review context in perioperative care for pain clinic referral documentation; no decision is generated.

source.title: Surgery and Opioids: Best Practice Guidelines 2021
source.population: People undergoing surgery, including people with chronic pain or preoperative opioid use.
source.setting: Preoperative assessment, perioperative planning, PACU, discharge, pain service, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting chronic pain, current opioid use, medicine and psychosocial or substance history, functional pain assessment, clinician-entered perioperative plan, complex pain review, discharge communication, and pain-service follow-up. Medication changes, doses, and tapering are not mapped; scheduled review is overdue.

exact_location.heading: Recovery, discharge, pain service, and follow-up
exact_location.locator: PDF PACU, discharge, and follow-up recommendations

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports functional pain assessment, complex pain or pain-service review, discharge communication, and clinician-arranged follow-up without autonomous treatment.
